_meta.description: 10 manufacturing process stage definitions with QC checklist templates for each RefrigiWear product category
_meta.usage: Use this file to render stage-specific inspection checklists in SankalpHub.in

development_samples.label: Development Samples
development_samples.description: Initial prototype review. Establish material, construction, and performance specs. All defect prevention starts here.
development_samples.objective: Validate all design, material, and construction specs before committing to production. Catch issues at lowest cost.
key_activities: Review proto against tech pack
Lab test materials (fabric, insulation, hardware)
Conduct fit evaluation across base size
Approve seam types, SPI, and thread specs
Validate special features (DWR, HiVis tape, BOA)
Sign off on all labels and compliance requirements
Issue comments and corrections to factory

mens_outerwear.item: Shell fabric denier and weave matches spec (400D nylon for Iron-Tuff®)
Insulation weight per panel matches spec (11.25 oz for Iron-Tuff®, 9 oz for PolarForce®)
DWR spray test rating ≥ 80 (AATCC 22) on shell fabric
Shade matches approved colorway lab-dip standard
Zipper brand, gauge, and type confirmed (YKK #10 brass for Iron-Tuff®)
Zipper operates smoothly — 20-cycle test
Seam strength meets minimum spec (≥200N grab test)
Seam type correct per tech pack (bound seams on Iron-Tuff®)
Knit cuff attachment strength — pull test passes
Brass rivets properly set — pull test ≥ 50N on each
HiVis reflective tape retroreflectivity ≥ 330 cd/lux/m² (HiVis styles only)
ANSI/ISEA 107 compliance confirmed for HiVis styles
All required labels present: brand, care, size, country of origin
Insulation distribution even — light table and shake test
Fit evaluation — overall silhouette, mobility, and coverage

womens_outerwear.item: Women's pattern grading correct — contoured hip, shoulder, and waist fit
Suspender strap width and length appropriate for women's sizing
All shell and insulation specs same as Men's equiv. Iron-Tuff® spec
Fit evaluation with female fit model across XS–XL

footwear.item: Upper material is waterproof-rated (hydrostatic head test)
Thinsulate insulation weight confirmed per model (200g spec)
Outsole compound batch test — slip resistance meets ASTM F2913 min 0.40
Outsole bond strength — peel test ≥ 4 N/mm full perimeter
BOA® dial installation per BOA guide — 200-cycle test (BOA styles)
Last is correct (Men's or Women's specific last)
Welt stitching SPI and thread type confirmed
Comfort rating temp test validated (-60°F for Extreme line)

gloves.item: Cut resistance yarn achieves ANSI A4 per TDM-100 (cut-resistant models)
Insulation weight and distribution confirmed — light table
Palm leather/material adhesion — peel test passes
Touch-Rite™ / Key-Rite™ nib placement correct — functional test
TPR impact pad bond strength ≥ 30N (Impact Pro styles)
ANSI/ISEA 105 rating confirmed by accredited lab

headwear.item: Acrylic yarn pill rating ≥ Grade 3 (Martindale ISO 12945-2)
HiVis color meets ANSI/ISEA 107 photometric standard
Sherpa lining shed test — max acceptable fiber count per 10 brush passes
Snap fastener size and type confirmed — 200-cycle test (Snap-On Hood)
Snap-On Hood mates correctly to Iron-Tuff® collar snap positions

accessories.item: Pallet cover moisture barrier — cold-flex test at -40°F passes
Handle stitching strength — 20kg hang test for 10 min

size_set.label: Size Set
size_set.description: Size set samples produced across full size range. Verify pattern grading accuracy and material consistency across sizes.
size_set.objective: Confirm all sizes fit correctly and materials are consistent across size run before PP sample stage.
key_activities: Produce samples in all offered sizes
Measure all garments against spec sheet measurements
Evaluate fit on fit models at min, mid, and max sizes
Verify insulation / material scales correctly with size
Check all hardware (zippers, snaps, BOA) across sizes
Issue measurement comments

mens_outerwear.item: Measure all sizes against spec sheet — chest, waist, sleeve, body length (tolerance ±1.5cm)
Verify insulation weight is correctly scaled per size pattern piece
Zipper length matches spec per size — no zip too short at large sizes
HiVis tape placement proportional across all sizes (distance from hem)
Dye lot consistent across entire size run
Size label correct and placed at correct position

footwear.item: Last size grading correct — half-size and full-size increments
BOA cable length verified per size (different cable per size range)
Outsole profile consistent across size run

gloves.item: Glove measurements per size (hand circumference range correct per size label)
Finger length and width scale correctly with size

headwear.item: Head circumference fits correctly for S/M and L/XL size bands

pp_samples.label: PP Samples
pp_samples.description: Pre-Production samples produced using bulk materials and production processes. Final approval gate before mass production begins.
pp_samples.objective: Confirm production-ready quality using bulk materials and locked production SOPs. No bulk production until PP approved.
key_activities: Produce using exact bulk fabric, hardware, and trims
Full measurement check vs. spec sheet
Full construction and quality audit
Function tests (zip, DWR, BOA, snap, reflective tape)
Compliance label and certification verification
Wash test on all washable products
Issue final approval or conditional approval comments
Lock all production SOPs before bulk starts

mens_outerwear.item: PP sample produced from exact bulk fabric lot and BOM components
Full measurement audit vs. spec sheet (all points, all sizes represented)
Seam strength test — all stress seams ≥ 200N (ASTM D5034)
Zipper: 50-cycle operation + 15-lb pull test
DWR spray test ≥ 80 on finished garment (AATCC 22)
Insulation shake test and light-table cold spot check
HiVis retroreflectometry ≥ 330 cd/lux/m² (HiVis styles)
Wash test (5 cycles) — check insulation migration, color fastness, label legibility
All labels present, correct, and compliant
Overall aesthetics approved — appearance, workmanship, presentation

footwear.item: Full construction audit — outsole bond (flex 5,000 cycles + peel test)
BOA 200-cycle test — ambient and -40°F if available
Slip resistance test (ASTM F2913 ≥ 0.40)
Cold temperature flex test on upper material

gloves.item: ANSI 105 cut test on production lot (accredited lab result on file)
Flex + peel test on palm material bond
Touch-Rite™/Key-Rite™ functional device test (touchscreen/keypad styles)

material.label: Material / Fabric
material.description: Incoming material inspection. All fabrics, insulation, hardware, trims, and labels inspected before they enter production.
material.objective: Prevent defective materials from reaching the production floor. Incoming QC is the last line of defense before production starts.
key_activities: Inspect incoming fabric rolls (4-point or 10-point system)
Weigh insulation per roll vs spec
Test DWR on shell fabric samples
Shade/color check against approved standard
Verify hardware (zippers, snaps, BOA, rivets) correct spec
Check labels against approved artwork
Maintain incoming material records per lot

mens_outerwear.item: Shell fabric denier verified (400D nylon per lab test or supplier COA)
Shade check — all rolls vs approved standard under D65 light (ΔE ≤ 1.0)
DWR spray test on 3 samples per roll (AATCC 22, min 80 rating)
Insulation roll weight per linear meter vs 11.25 oz/yd² spec (±5%)
Visual roll inspection — no holes, contamination, weave defects (4-point system)
HiVis fabric spectrophotometric test — meets ANSI CIE coordinates (HiVis styles)
Zipper incoming: 5% random operation test, teeth alignment check
3M Scotchlite tape: retroreflective surface check, adhesive strip test
Labels: content matches approved artwork, print legible, correct quantity
Thread: correct ticket number, color, and tensile COA verified

footwear.item: Upper material waterproof rating test (hydrostatic head)
Adhesive cement: correct brand/batch, within pot life date
BOA components: correct model, function test 5% random sample
Outsole compound: visual check for contamination

gloves.item: Cut-resistant yarn COA verified — every incoming lot. ANSI A4 rated.
Palm leather: skive thickness check (0.8mm spec)
Insulation: weight per unit area vs spec

headwear.item: Acrylic yarn: denier and twist count vs spec (min 32-count)
HiVis yarn: spectrophotometric reading per lot
Snap fasteners: size, material (metal vs plastic), finish

cutting.label: Cutting
cutting.description: Fabric cutting operation. Panel accuracy, grain line alignment, and dye lot segregation are critical at this stage.
cutting.objective: Ensure all cut panels are accurate, correctly marked, and properly organized to prevent downstream defects.
key_activities: Lay-up fabric per spreading plan
Verify grain line alignment before cutting
Check panel dimensions against master pattern (first piece)
Segregate panels by dye lot
Mark and bundle by style, size, and dye lot
Inspect cut edges for accuracy and completeness

mens_outerwear.item: Grain line alignment within ±3mm on all shell panels
Cut panel dimensions match master pattern (first piece ±3mm)
Dye lot number recorded and marked on each bundle
No panels cut from different dye lots mixed in one bundle
Insulation panels cut to correct size — no compression under cutter
Cut edges clean — no fraying or blade drag marks on nylon shell
HiVis panels: no dirt or oil contamination on tape application areas
Panel count per bundle matches cut plan (correct pcs per size)

footwear.item: Upper pattern pieces cut accurately to lasting margin spec
Lasting margin consistency — uniform width around perimeter

gloves.item: Cut-resistant shell panels correctly cut — no stretching or distortion
Cut-resistant and standard panels NOT mixed in same bundle
Fourchettes (side fingers) cut to correct taper spec

sewing_stitching.label: Sewing / Stitching
sewing_stitching.description: Main sewing and stitching operations. SPI, seam type, thread tension, and operator technique directly affect product quality.
sewing_stitching.objective: Produce all seams, hems, and stitching to spec. Detect operator and machine issues early.
key_activities: First piece approval before each bundle
SPI count on all seam types
Seam strength grab test on first piece
Thread tension calibration check
Needle change schedule (every 8 hours)
Seam type matches tech pack (bound seams where required)
Skip stitch monitoring on all hem and cuff seams

mens_outerwear.item: SPI count: 10–12 on nylon shell seams (per approved spec)
Seam type matches tech pack spec — bound seams on Iron-Tuff® shell
First piece seam grab test ≥ 200N (ASTM D5034)
Thread tension balanced — no thread looping, puckering, or skipping
Correct needle type and size for 400D nylon (Nm 90/14)
Needle change record — every 8 hours operational time
Hem stitch: no skip stitches on cuff or body hem (visual + 10x loupe)
Pocket seam: correct position (±5mm), fully enclosed bag
Label insertion at correct position and orientation
Bar tack placement at all stress points (pocket corners, underarm)
Zipper installation: tape flat, no puckering, chain free of debris
Knit cuff attachment: 4-thread safety stitch, no puckering or gaping

footwear.item: Lasting stitching SPI 8–10, waxed polyester thread confirmed
Upper stitching skip-free — no skip stitches on upper vamp seam
Welt stitching: tension and SPI correct, no skip stitches in welt channel

gloves.item: Finger crotch seam: narrow seam allowance (4mm), no puckering
Palm-to-back seam: correct SPI and no gaping at seam allowance
Cuff hem: even width, no skip stitches

headwear.item: Knit cap crown seam: even tension, no visible needle holes
Balaclava face opening: hem is even, no stretched edge

assembly.label: Assembly
assembly.description: Component assembly, hardware attachment, special feature application (tape, BOA, rivets, snaps). Final construction before inline QC.
assembly.objective: Correctly assemble all components and verify all special features function correctly before end-of-line inspection.
key_activities: Zipper attachment and stop setting
Reflective tape heat-press application
Brass rivet setting
BOA dial installation
Snap fastener attachment
Suspender strap assembly
Pocket bag assembly
Hood and collar attachment
Outsole cementing and pressing (footwear)

mens_outerwear.item: Reflective tape: heat-press temp within spec (±5°C), dwell time correct (HiVis styles)
Reflective tape: fully flat, zero edge lift or air bubbles on 100% check
Tape position vs. spec: ±10mm tolerance on placement measurements
Brass rivets: fully set, no sharp edges exposed, pull test ≥ 50N
Zipper: storm flap clearance check, top/bottom stops set correctly
Insulation distribution: light-table check post-assembly before closing seam
Hood (Ice Parka): sewn-on hood fully attached, drawstring functional
Suspender clips (bibs): load test 50 lbs, 10-cycle release test
Snap-On Hood snaps: all snaps correct size and aligned to collar spec
Pocket bags: fully enclosed, correct depth per spec

footwear.item: Cement coverage: 100% outsole surface covered, no dry spots before pressing
Press machine: 60 PSI pressure, 60-second dwell confirmed per pair
BOA installation: dial orientation correct, cable routing per diagram
Outsole perimeter: zero edge lift immediately after pressing

gloves.item: TPR impact pads: heat-activated adhesive correct, all pads flush
Velcro cuff: bonded and/or sewn, 5 open/close test
Insulation: tacking complete, no loose areas before glove closed

headwear.item: Snap fasteners on hood: all snaps attached at correct positions
HiVis reversible knit cap: both layers correctly joined, no missed stitches

inline_inspection.label: Inline Inspection
inline_inspection.description: Real-time quality inspection during active production. Catch defects at the point of production to minimize rework and loss.
inline_inspection.objective: Detect and correct quality issues while production is running. Every defect caught inline saves rework cost.
key_activities: Roving inspector checks each workstation
Defect rate tracking by operator and machine
Hourly defect tallies by defect type
First-piece check on every new bundle
100% functional checks (zip, BOA, tape)
Measurement audit every 50 pcs
Corrective action if defect rate exceeds 5%

mens_outerwear.item: Seam quality: SPI count + tug test on underarm/shoulder — every 50 pcs
Zipper: 10-cycle operation test on every garment (100%)
Insulation: light-table cold spot check + shake test — every 50 pcs
HiVis tape: tactile edge-adhesion check on every garment (100%)
DWR: spray test on 5 pcs per hour post-finishing
Measurements: spot-check chest, body length, sleeve every 50 pcs
Labels: all labels present and correct — every 25 pcs
Shade: spot shade check under D65 light every 2 hours
Rivets: tactile check all rivet positions — every 50 pcs
Workmanship: overall garment appearance vs approved sample every 25 pcs

footwear.item: Outsole perimeter: tactile check every pair for edge lift
BOA: 10-cycle operation test every pair (100%)
Upper stitching: visual check for skip stitches every 10 pairs

gloves.item: Insulation: tactile finger check on every glove for cold spots
Seam check: finger crotch and palm seam every 50 pairs
Touch-Rite™/Key-Rite™: functional device test every 25 pairs
Yarn identity: burn test spot check every 100 pairs (cut-resistant)

headwear.item: Shade check under D65 — every 50 pcs (HiVis especially)
Snap function test every 25 pcs (Snap-On Hood)

final_inspection.label: Final Inspection
final_inspection.description: Pre-shipment AQL inspection on completed, packed or ready-to-pack production. Pass/fail decision for shipment release.
final_inspection.objective: AQL-based statistical sampling of finished goods. Issue pass or fail decision. Document all findings for traceability.
key_activities: Draw AQL sample per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4
Full inspection of each sampled piece
Functional tests (zip, BOA, DWR, tape)
Measurement audit vs spec sheet
Label and compliance audit (100% in sample)
Workmanship and appearance evaluation
Issue final inspection report
Pass/Fail decision per AQL levels

mens_outerwear.item: Seam strength: grab test (destructive) on 5% of sample — ≥ 200N
Zipper: 10-cycle + 15-lb pull test on all sampled garments
DWR spray test (AATCC 22) on all sampled garments — min 80 rating
Insulation: light-table + shake test on all sampled garments
HiVis tape: retroreflectometer on 10% sample + peel test all sampled
ANSI/ISEA 107 compliance label present on all HiVis garments (100%)
All labels: care, size, brand, COO on all sampled garments (100% in sample)
Measurements: all critical dimensions vs spec sheet (tolerance ±1.5cm)
Shade: all sampled garments vs approved shade bar under D65 light
Workmanship: overall appearance, cleanliness, no stains or damage
Brass rivets: pull test ≥ 50N on 10% of sample
Suspender clip load test 50 lbs on 10% of bib sample

footwear.item: Outsole bond: flex 500 cycles + perimeter peel test on sample
BOA: 20-cycle + 10-lb cable pull test on all sampled pairs
Slip resistance: ASTM F2913 ≥ 0.40 (1 pair per size per shipment)
Pair matching: left/right size and color match on all sampled pairs

gloves.item: Cut resistance: destructive TDM-100 test on 1% of lot
Insulation: tactile cold spot + light-table check on all sampled gloves
Touch-Rite™/Key-Rite™ nibs: functional device test on 100% of sample
ANSI certification label present on all cut-resistant gloves

headwear.item: HiVis color: spectrophotometric or retroreflectometric test on sample
Snap: mate to Iron-Tuff® collar sample — 100-cycle test on snap-on hoods

packing.label: Packing
packing.description: Final packing, labeling, and carton operations. Correct packing protects product and ensures accurate order fulfillment.
packing.objective: Ensure every carton is correctly packed, labeled, and prepared for shipment without damaging the finished product.
key_activities: Match packing list to actual garments
Attach hang tags correctly
Fold and bag per approved packing method
Seal polybags
Pack cartons per assortment plan
Barcode scan verification
Carton label check (style, size, PO, destination)
Carton weight and dimension check
Shipping mark verification

mens_outerwear.item: Garment is clean, pressed if needed, and damage-free before bagging
Main zipper fully closed before folding
Hang tag attached — correct style, size, UPC barcode scans correctly
Polybag size label matches garment size label
Reflective tape protected — tissue paper between layers (HiVis styles)
Folding method matches approved packing standard (not creasing tape/hardware)
Carton contents match packing list (style, size, color, quantity)
Carton label: PO#, style#, size ratio, destination port correct
Carton gross weight within specified range (±0.5 kg)
Carton sealing tape applied — all edges sealed, no open flaps

footwear.item: Pairs matched L/R before boxing
24-hr cure time respected before boxing (for cemented boots)
BOA dial facing inward in shoe box to avoid transit damage
Toe stuffing in place for boot shape retention

gloves.item: Pairs matched by size and style before packaging
ANSI certification visible on hang tag or label after packing

headwear.item: HiVis items not compressed under heavy weight in cartons
